name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of experience in analytical method development, impurity-focused characterization, and quality-oriented process support for small-molecule and advanced materials systems. Expertise spans chromatographic and spectroscopic analysis including HPLC, GC-MS, GPC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, and NMR, with a strong record of technical reporting, cross-functional problem solving, and method documentation. Experienced in translating analytical findings into scale-up, specification-setting, and process-development decisions.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Leading analytical method development for polymer process-development programs, designing HPLC, GPC, and spectroscopic workflows to monitor reaction progress, assess product purity, and establish fit-for-purpose quality specifications for scale-up.

Developing stability-indicating thermal-analysis methods (TGA, DSC) and structured characterization plans to diagnose degradation behavior, compare material variants, and support technical decision making.
Applying DOE-driven experimentation to identify critical process parameters, improve reproducibility, and translate analytical data into practical process-control recommendations.

Developed and executed multi-technique analytical characterization workflows across 15+ organic and organometallic compound variants, using FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, cyclic voltammetry, spectroelectrochemistry, and ellipsometry to evaluate structure-property-performance relationships.

Designed accelerated stability and degradation studies, interpreting impurity and failure signatures to guide material selection, processing choices, and troubleshooting discussions with cross-functional collaborators.
Authored detailed technical reports and experimental summaries that translated analytical findings into clear recommendations for chemists and engineers.

Co-led AFOSR-funded process development for molecular thin-film coatings, developing surface preparation protocols and CVD deposition strategies with in-situ analytical monitoring to achieve controlled film morphology and reproducible device performance.

Developed and optimized polymer thin-film deposition processes using DOE strategies and in-situ QCM-D process monitoring, establishing process control parameters and characterizing film growth kinetics to improve coating reproducibility.

Managed analytical instrumentation and laboratory operations for a 15-member research group; implemented equipment tracking, calibration, and SOP systems that improved instrument uptime and strengthened documentation discipline across shared workflows.

skills.Analytical Techniques: HPLC, GC-MS, GPC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, NMR, TGA, DSC, ellipsometry, cyclic voltammetry, spectroelectrochemistry, QCM-D
skills.Method Development: Analytical method development and validation, impurity profiling, specification setting, stability testing, assay development, root-cause troubleshooting
skills.Statistical Tools: DOE, Statistical Process Control (SPC), JMP (basic), Python (basic), Excel (advanced), Weibull analysis (familiarity)
skills.Quality & Regulatory: cGMP awareness, SOP development, data integrity principles, technical documentation, cross-functional communication, method-transfer readiness
skills.Materials Expertise: Small molecule characterization, polymer analysis, thin-film materials, organic/organometallic compounds, surface analysis

projects.title: Analytical Method Development for Quality-Focused Process Support
projects.description: Led development of chromatographic and spectroscopic methods for monitoring synthesis, characterizing product quality, and defining fit-for-purpose specifications for novel material systems.
projects.tech: HPLC, GPC, FT-IR, TGA, DSC, DOE

projects.title: Impurity and Degradation Pattern Assessment Across Material Variants
projects.description: Designed systematic analytical evaluations across multiple material variants to identify degradation pathways, interpret failure signatures, and inform process-development decisions.
projects.tech: UV-Vis-NIR, FT-IR, Cyclic Voltammetry, Statistical Analysis

projects.title: AFOSR Thin-Film Coatings Process Development
projects.description: Co-led process development for defense optical coating application, developing deposition protocols with analytical monitoring to ensure consistent film quality and performance.

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
